goal: Implement execution flow tracing: BFS from entry points through forward CALLS edges, creating Process nodes with STEP_IN_PROCESS edges, exposed via ContextGraph.flows() and CLI graph-flows command

tasks.name: Implement flow tracing phase
microSteps: Create tests/test_context_flows.py with tests: empty graph returns empty, single entry point traces forward calls, multi-hop BFS respects max_depth, multiple entry points produce distinct Process nodes, STEP_IN_PROCESS edges connect process to each step with depth property, cycles in call graph don't cause infinite loop
Run tests to verify RED (flows module doesn't exist)
Create scripts/context/phases/flows.py with FlowResult dataclass and trace_flows() function
Implement entry point detection reusing _is_entry_point logic from dead_code.py, plus if-__name__-main guard detection
Implement forward BFS from each entry point through CALLS edges, creating Process node and STEP_IN_PROCESS edges at each depth
Persist Process nodes and STEP_IN_PROCESS edges to storage
Run tests to verify GREEN
tasks.action: Create the flow tracing phase module. FlowResult should contain: process_id, entry_point (GraphNode), steps (list of FlowStep with node, depth). trace_flows(storage, max_depth=10) finds all entry points, runs forward BFS through CALLS edges for each, creates Process nodes (NodeLabel.PROCESS) with STEP_IN_PROCESS edges. Entry point detection: reuse dead_code._is_entry_point() heuristic (main, test_*, Test*, __init__.py, is_entry_point flag). Persist Process nodes and edges to storage. Handle cycles via visited set.

tasks.name: Wire flows() into ContextGraph and index pipeline
microSteps: Add test in tests/test_context_graph.py for flows() method: index a repo with entry points, call flows(), verify Process nodes and STEP_IN_PROCESS edges exist
Run test to verify RED
Import FlowResult and trace_flows in scripts/context/__init__.py
Add flows(max_depth=10) method to ContextGraph class that calls trace_flows(self._storage, max_depth)
Add trace_flows call to end of index() pipeline (after heritage, before file hash update)
Update __all__ export list
Run tests to verify GREEN
tasks.action: Wire trace_flows into ContextGraph: import FlowResult and trace_flows from phases.flows, add flows(max_depth=10) method, add trace_flows() call at end of index() pipeline. Update __all__.

tasks.name: Add graph-flows CLI subcommand
microSteps: Add TestGraphFlows class in tests/test_context_cli.py with tests: help, empty repo, indexed repo shows flows, --json output with expected keys, --max-depth flag
Run tests to verify RED
Add cmd_graph_flows() function in workflow_memory.py following cmd_graph_communities pattern
Register graph-flows subparser with --repo, --json, --max-depth arguments
Add graph-flows to _graph_cmds set and dispatch dict
Run tests to verify GREEN
tasks.action: Add graph-flows CLI subcommand to workflow_memory.py. Human-readable output: list each flow with entry point and step count. JSON output: list of {process_id, entry_point: {name, file_path, label}, steps: [{name, file_path, label, depth}]}. Add --max-depth flag (default 10). Register in _graph_cmds and dispatch.

commitMessage: feat(context-graph): add execution flow tracing with Process nodes, STEP_IN_PROCESS edges, and graph-flows CLI
